title: nasturtium nasturtium-aquaticum
intro: Nasturtium nasturtium-aquaticum, also known as watercress or nasturtium, is a plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its oval-shaped leaves and peppery flavor. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to its high content of vitamins A, C, and E, as well as its rich mineral profile. The health benefits of nasturtium-aquaticum include its ability to reduce inflammation, boost the immune system, and protect against certain types of cancer, making it a popular ingredient in herbal preparations such as teas, salads, and juices. Herbal preparations of nasturtium-aquaticum can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is also used as a garnish or added to salads, sandwiches, and smoothies.
